text.title: facebook-ads Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for facebook-ads. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: All budget values are in cents: daily_budget of 10000 equals $100.00. Always verify the unit before setting budgets.
rule.rationale: Setting budget in dollars instead of cents causes 100x overspend. This is the most common Meta API mistake.

rule.statement: Always create campaigns with status PAUSED. Activate only after reviewing all ad sets and creatives are correctly configured.
rule.rationale: A campaign set to ACTIVE immediately starts spending. PAUSED allows review without risk.

rule.statement: Use LOWEST_COST_WITHOUT_CAP for initial campaigns without a known target CPA. Switch to COST_CAP or LOWEST_COST_WITH_MIN_ROAS only after establishing baseline performance.
rule.rationale: Cap-based strategies require accurate cost knowledge to set. Without baseline data, caps that are too low starve the algorithm of learning budget.

rule.statement: Match optimization_goal to billing_event: OFFSITE_CONVERSIONS uses IMPRESSIONS billing; LINK_CLICKS uses IMPRESSIONS billing. Mismatched pairs cause ad set rejection.
rule.rationale: Meta validates goal/billing combinations server-side. Rejected ad sets fail silently unless you check the API error response.

rule.statement: Check video upload status before creating the creative. Creating a creative with a video in non-ready state returns a validation error.
rule.rationale: Video processing is asynchronous and can take minutes. Polling is required; there is no webhook for upload completion in the basic API.

rule.statement: Name campaigns, ad sets, and ads with a consistent pattern that encodes objective, audience, and date so performance can be compared across periods without pulling metadata.
rule.rationale: Ad account naming chaos makes bulk optimization and historical comparison extremely slow.
